Title: High-quality haplotype-resolved chromosome assembly provides evolutionary insights and targeted steviol glycosides (SGs) biosynthesis in Stevia rebaudiana Bertoni

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Him Stevia; CSIR-IHBT-ST-04; 10 diverse stevia genotypes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product and further glycosylates it to downstream rebaudiosides
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylates stevioside (Stv) to form rebaudioside A (Reb-A), and further glycosylates Reb-A toward Reb-D and Reb-M
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: rebaudioside A; rebaudioside D; rebaudioside M
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Five tandemly duplicated copies on chromosome 8 showed higher expression in high Reb-A cultivar; four copies (UGT76G1.13/g66507, UGT76G1.15/g66505, UGT76G1.17/g66518, UGT76G1.18/g66515) highly expressed in high Reb-A genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf-expressed; higher in 'Him Stevia' and high Reb-A cultivars than in low Reb-A cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of stevioside to Reb-A and contributes to higher Reb-A accumulation; allelic and copy-number/expression differences associate with targeted SG composition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression correlation; qRT-PCR across 10 genotypes; haplotype-resolved structural/allelic analysis; transcriptome variant calling; resequencing validation; homology modeling and molecular docking
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control enzyme for Reb-A diversification, but formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MYB; WRKY; bZIP; bHLH transcription factor families (predicted regulatory interactions)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly and annotation; RNA-seq (in-house and public PRJNA591974; PRJNA260244); resequencing of 10 genotypes (~25×); project accession PRJNA988897
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoters; seven copies contained MYB (MYB/MBS/MRE), WRKY (W-box), bZIP (AS-1) and bHLH (G-box) cis-elements
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Multisubstrate functionality may also favor conversion of Reb-A to Reb-D/M, complicating targeted accumulation of a single glycoside
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate marker and engineering target for selective breeding/editing to increase Reb-A or tune downstream rebaudiosides; allele-aware editing of favorable copies/haplotypes is recommended
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Five tandem copies clustered within ~9.4 kb on chromosome 8; expression positively correlated with Reb-A (r^2=0.87) and weakly/negatively with Stv (r^2=-0.20). Missense variants Phe46Leu, Ala86Ser, Leu200Val and Asn385Thr in acceptor region alter substrate preference; Leu200Val may favor Reb-A to Reb-D/M, while others may favor Stv to Reb-A. Haplotype A-biased allele showed FC 4 upregulation in 'Him Stevia'
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tandemly duplicated UGT76G1 copies were expanded in the stevia genome and highly expressed in the high Reb-A cultivar. 2. UGT76G1 expression/copy number showed strong positive association with Reb-A content, identifying it as a major causal enzyme for targeted SG composition. 3. Haplotype-resolved and resequencing analyses identified missense variants and allele-biased expression that likely alter substrate preference and glycosylation outcomes.

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: OG0001040 family; specific copies include g54980; g54978; g54984
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Steviol backbone formation / SG diversification entry step
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream after ent-kaurenoic acid, entry to steviol formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms the first SG precursor steviol from ent-kaurenoic acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cytochrome P450-catalyzed oxidation of ent-kaurenoic acid to generate steviol, the first-generation SG backbone
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase; PF00067; CYP716 family
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Five gene copies showed differential expression in contrasting stevia genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf-expressed SG pathway gene family; one validated CYP716 assayed by qRT-PCR showed higher expression in high Reb-A genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation/diversification of the steviol backbone, enabling SG biosynthesis and contributing to differences between genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome-wide family identification; RNA-seq differential expression; GRN/promoter analysis; qRT-PCR of SG pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important enzyme significantly involved in SG diversification; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: B3; AP2/ERF; B3-ARF; bHLH; bZIP; C2C2-CO-like; C2C2-Dof; C2C2-GATA; C2H2; C3H; DBB; HB-HD-ZIP; HSF; MYB; MYB-related; NF-YA; NF-YB; S1Fa-like; Tify; Trihelix
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; RNA-seq; GRN using in-house transcriptomes plus PRJNA591974 and PRJNA260244; project accession PRJNA988897
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoters; specific copies g54980, g54978 and g54984 contained MYB-recognition/MYC, G-box and W-box cis-elements
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target with UGTs for metabolic engineering of overall SG flux and diversification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CYP716 comprised 27 copies, including 8 tandem duplicates over ~20 kb; considered important for SG diversification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP716 is an expanded and tandemly duplicated P450 family in stevia. 2. It catalyzes conversion of ent-kaurenoic acid to steviol, the entry point to SG biosynthesis. 3. Differential expression and TF-linked regulation suggest that specific CYP716 copies contribute to genotype-dependent SG accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for SG glycosylation and diversification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: UGT-mediated glycosylation step in steviol glycoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: One copy (g88064) showed notably higher expression in high Reb-A cultivars; 14 copies had significant TF interactions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf-expressed; elevated in high Reb-A cultivars by qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with high Reb-A accumulation and SG glycosylation capacity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome-wide family identification; GRN/promoter analysis; qRT-PCR across contrasting genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AP2/ERF; B3; bHLH; bZIP; C2C2-CO-like; C2C2-Dof; C2C2-GATA; C2H2; C3H; DBB; GRAS; HB-HD-ZIP; HSF; LIM; LOB; MYB; MYB-related; NF-YA; NF-YB; S1Fa-like; Tify; Trihelix; WRKY
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; RNA-seq; GRN using in-house transcriptomes plus PRJNA591974 and PRJNA260244; project accession PRJNA988897
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoters; eight copies harbored MYB, WRKY and bHLH binding sites
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for stacking with UGT76G1/CYP716 to fine-tune SG composition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expanded to 29 copies in the genome
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT85C2 is an expanded UGT family member implicated in SG glycosylation. 2. A tested copy showed higher expression in high Reb-A genotypes. 3. Multiple copies are embedded in TF regulatory networks linked to SG biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for SG glycosylation and diversification
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: UGT-mediated glycosylation step in steviol glycoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Three copies had significant TF interactions; qRT-PCR included UGT74G1 as SG pathway gene
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to SG glycosylation network associated with SG accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome-wide family identification; GRN/promoter analysis; qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AP2/ERF; B3-ARF; bHLH; bZIP; C2C2-CO-like; C2C2-Dof; C2C2-GATA; C2H2; C3H; DBB; HB-HD-ZIP; HSF; MYB; MYB-related; NF-YA; NF-YB; S1Fa-like
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; RNA-seq; GRN using in-house transcriptomes plus PRJNA591974 and PRJNA260244; project accession PRJNA988897
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoters; copies g82558 and g44153 contained MYB and bZIP binding sites
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate supporting SG pathway engineering in combination with other UGTs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expanded to 20 copies in the genome
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74G1 belongs to an expanded UGT family involved in SG glycosylation. 2. Multiple copies are integrated into SG-related TF regulatory networks. 3. Promoter cis-elements support transcriptional control linked to SG metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: MEP pathway feeding steviol glycoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool for SG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reductoisomerization step in the plastidial MEP pathway supplying isoprenoid precursors for steviol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Three gene copies identified; significantly higher expression in high Reb-A cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression associates with high Reb-A content by increasing precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq pathway expression; qRT-PCR across 10 genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream engineering target to boost SG precursor flux
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as one of key MEP backbone genes validated by qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DXR is part of the MEP precursor pathway for SG biosynthesis. 2. Its expression is higher in high Reb-A genotypes. 3. It likely supports increased SG accumulation through enhanced precursor supply.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: MEP pathway feeding steviol glycoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies immediate diterpene precursor pool for SG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces geranylgeranyl pyrophosphate, the diterpene precursor for steviol backbone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Nine gene copies identified; significantly higher expression in high Reb-A cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression associates with high Reb-A accumulation through increased diterpene precursor production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq pathway expression; qRT-PCR across 10 genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising flux-enhancing target for pathway engineering together with downstream CYP/UGT genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of expanded MEP backbone gene set
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GGPPS supplies GGPP precursor for steviol biosynthesis. 2. It is more highly expressed in high Reb-A genotypes. 3. It likely contributes to enhanced SG production by increasing pathway flux.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for steviol formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Single gene identified; significantly higher expression in high Reb-A cultivars
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression associates with increased SG precursor formation and high Reb-A genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq pathway expression; qRT-PCR across 10 genotypes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful backbone-pathway target to increase flux toward SG biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Validated as a key MEP/SG backbone gene in qRT-PCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KO catalyzes formation of ent-kaurenoic acid, a pivotal SG precursor. 2. KO expression is elevated in high Reb-A cultivars. 3. It likely supports enhanced SG biosynthesis by increasing backbone precursor availability.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CYP716; UGT85C2; UGT74G1; UGT76G1 copies with MYB-binding motifs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Predicted to regulate SG pathway genes through MYB-binding cis-elements
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SG pathway genes in GRN; MYB-recognition motif-containing promoters
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in high Reb-A cultivars
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf qRT-PCR higher in high Reb-A cultivars
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with high Reb-A accumulation as a putative positive regulator of SG biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GRN prediction; promoter cis-element analysis; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Potential hub-like positive regulator implicated in high Reb-A genotypes
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq-based GRN using in-house transcriptomes plus PRJNA591974 and PRJNA260244; project accession PRJNA988897
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulatory target for coordinated activation of SG pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper identifies MYB family among major regulators; MYB102 specifically validated by qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB102 is highly expressed in high Reb-A genotypes. 2. MYB-family binding motifs are enriched in promoters of key SG genes including CYP716 and UGTs. 3. MYB102 is a strong candidate positive regulator for targeted SG biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CYP716; UGT85C2; UGT74G1; UGT76G1 copies with MYB-binding motifs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Predicted to regulate SG pathway genes through MYB-binding cis-elements
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SG pathway genes in GRN; MYB-recognition motif-containing promoters
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in high Reb-A cultivars
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf qRT-PCR higher in high Reb-A cultivars
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with high Reb-A accumulation as a putative positive regulator of SG biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GRN prediction; promoter cis-element analysis; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Potential positive regulator linked to high Reb-A trait
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq-based GRN using in-house transcriptomes plus PRJNA591974 and PRJNA260244; project accession PRJNA988897
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for coordinated transcriptional engineering of SG biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MYB family highlighted as key TF class in GRN
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB73 shows elevated expression in high Reb-A genotypes. 2. MYB motifs are present in promoters of major SG genes. 3. MYB73 is implicated as a candidate transcriptional activator of SG accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: CYP716; UGT85C2; UGT74G1; UGT76G1 copies with G-box motifs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Predicted to regulate SG pathway genes through G-box-containing promoters
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Possible TF combinatorial regulation with MYB not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SG pathway genes in GRN; G-box motif-containing promoters
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in high Reb-A cultivars
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf qRT-PCR higher in high Reb-A cultivars
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with high Reb-A accumulation as a candidate positive regulator of SG pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GRN prediction; promoter cis-element analysis; qRT-PCR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Potential central regulator because bHLH interactions were found for multiple core SG genes
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq-based GRN using in-house transcriptomes plus PRJNA591974 and PRJNA260244; project accession PRJNA988897
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate TF for regulatory engineering, potentially combined with MYB regulators
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: bHLH family was identified among key potential regulators in summary and GRN
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bHLH TF expression is higher in high Reb-A genotypes. 2. G-box cis-elements occur in promoters of CYP716 and several UGT copies. 3. bHLH is implicated as a broad positive regulator of SG biosynthesis.
